title: ophrys sphegodes subsp. grammica
intro: Ophrys sphegodes subsp. grammica, also known as the Early Spider Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe, characterized by its unique, long-spurred flowers that resemble a female spider. The Early Spider Orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, primarily due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of this plant have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which provide antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. In herbal preparations, the Early Spider Orchid is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and eczema, as well as to soothe and calm the skin.
